steps: Fake founder submits interest, opens support topic proposal, receives admin answer.
expected: Proposal and accountability item use sample data only.

steps: Sample business owner submits stage, sector, support-priority survey.
expected: No ABN, revenue, client, tax, contract, or legal fields exist.

steps: Fake participant RSVPs and facilitator publishes public-safe minutes.
expected: Minutes omit attendee identity and private support detail.

steps: Mentor tries to access private founder notes before approval.
Partner observer opens public updates and attempts private export.
expected: Observer cannot see private exports or participant records.

steps: Dean sample reviewer logs a public build finding.
expected: Finding cannot approve pilot or override authority.

steps: Fake action is assigned, updated, and closed.
expected: Closeout evidence remains public-safe status only.

steps: Sample member opens welcome path and official boundary page.
expected: Boundary states official records remain outside Decidim.

steps: Guest views meeting info and attempts member-only action.
expected: Guest cannot access member-only actions or official route.

steps: Facilitator creates agenda with speaker, evaluator, timer, chair roles.
expected: Agenda has fake names and public-safe minutes only.
Only aggregate sample result is visible publicly.

steps: Tester searches for member number, payment, signature, charter evidence.
expected: No official records exist in Decidim pages, CSV, JSON, or screenshots.

steps: Sample member creates a club idea or motion.
expected: Copy says it is not an official Toastmasters decision.

steps: Safety reviewer triggers fake pause and support route.
expected: Public page shows pause state without affected-person detail.

steps: Temporary admin is invited, used, revoked, and re-tested.
expected: Revoked account cannot access admin area.

steps: Scan pages, docs, CSV, JSON, and screenshots for private-data terms.
expected: No real records or official Toastmasters data are present.

steps: Technical custodian proves backup, export, deletion, and restore in private environment.
expected: Only version labels and pass/fail summary become public.

steps: Review all issues and evidence, then choose fake go/hold/no-go outcome.
expected: Real launch remains blocked until private approvals pass.
